summary: Clean implementation with minimal logic. One redundant test (test 3 duplicates test 1). The dropped source field from resolveAuth is an implicit design decision with no test documenting it.

suggestions.description: Test "prefers session over push when resolveAuth returns session" is redundant with the first test
suggestions.rationale: Both tests mock resolveAuth to return a session token and assert teamId/projectId are included. The name implies priority logic exists in buildPushAuth, but it does not — resolveAuth handles that. This test adds no coverage and its name is misleading about what buildPushAuth does.
suggestions.suggestion: Remove this test entirely. The first test already covers session token behavior. If the intent is to test resolveAuth priority, that belongs in the auth module tests.

suggestions.description: Unknown token type test only checks teamId/projectId, not the full output shape
suggestions.rationale: Tests 1 and 2 use toEqual for full shape verification. Test 5 only checks two fields. This inconsistency means token and tokenType propagation for unknown tokens is untested.
suggestions.suggestion: Use toEqual with the full expected object for consistency, or at minimum also assert result.tokenType to verify it passes through the unknown type.

suggestions.description: No test documents that the source field from resolveAuth is intentionally excluded from PushAuthConfig
suggestions.rationale: resolveAuth returns { token, source, tokenType } but buildPushAuth drops source. This is a design decision that has no test documenting it. A future refactor could accidentally include source without any test catching the shape change.
suggestions.suggestion: Add an assertion in one of the existing tests that the result does NOT contain a source property, e.g. expect(result).not.toHaveProperty("source"). This documents the deliberate exclusion.
